intro:
Unfortunately, I was unable to verify information about Lomanthus cerrateae.

However, I was able to verify information about Lomatium, a genus of plants that includes Lomatium ceratoides and Lomatium occidentale among others. 

Lomatium (or Lomanthus) is a genus of flowering plants in the carrot family (Apiaceae). Lomatium is a perennial plant native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. It has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments.

Some of the health benefits associated with Lomatium include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which may help to treat respiratory infections, colds, and flu. 

The bioactive constituents of Lomatium include alkaloids, volatile oils, and other compounds. Some of the specific bioactive compounds found in Lomatium include sesquiterpenes, phenolic acids, and flavonoids.

Lomatium is most commonly consumed as a tea, made by infusing the dried root or flowers in hot water. It can also be found in capsule or tincture form.
